calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-25-2019 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jan-03-2018 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Mar-26-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-11-2017 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Dec-06-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Daniel L. Feder of the law offices of Daniel Louis Feder (415-391-9471) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

No other appearance is made in this matter

There being no appearance by moving party and the Court having received an email from moving party indicating no appearance, the Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT Y. GREGG HIGUCHI, O.D., "EYE GOTCHA", YUKIO HIGUCHI, Y. GREGG HIGUCHI, O.D. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication.

Defendants' motion for summary judgment is denied and their alternative motion for summary adjudication is granted on the twelfth cause of action for invasion of privacy and denied as to all other causes of action. There are triable disputes precluding summary adjudication in favor of defendants on Ms. Cahayag's first through fourth and eighth through tenth causes of action for failure to accommodate and discrimination whether: 1) the January 26, 2017 and February 22, 2016 letters from Dr. Thiet should have been read together by defendants; 2) reading those letters together put defendants on notice that Dr. Thiet informed defendants that Ms. Cahayag was disabled by postpartum complications which prevented her from doing any of the work required by her job until April 2, 2016; 3) reading those letters together made it obvious what disability Ms. Cahayag had and that there was no accommodation that could be made for that disability other than full leave until April 2, 2016; 4) reading those letters together made it unnecessary and inappropriate for defendants to inquire about Ms. Cahayag's disability and her ability to perform her job duties; and 5) Ms. Cahayag was entitled to be on disability leave until April 2, 2016 and terminating her employment before that date was unlawful. In addition to the previously stated triable disputes, there is a triable dispute precluding summary adjudication in favor of defendants on Ms. Cahayag's fifth, sixth and eleventh causes for retaliation whether defendants' terminated Ms. Cahayag's employment because Ms. Cahayag exercised her right to request that she be on leave until April 2, 2016 due to her disability based on postpartum complications. In addition to the previously stated triable disputes, there are triable disputes precluding summary adjudication on Ms. Cahayag's seventh cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress whether: 1) requesting Ms. Cahayag to return to work when she had a disability that prevented her from performing the duties of job and terminating Ms. Cahayag's employment while she was entitled to be on disability leave constitute outrageous conduct and 2) Ms. Cahayag suffered severe emotional distress as a result of being pressured to return to work and having her employment terminated. Defendants are entitled to summary adjudication in their favor on Ms. Cahayag's twelfth cause of action for invasion of privacy because the undisputed facts establish that requesting that Ms. Cahayag's physician complete the questionnaire sent by the defendants is not highly offensive to a reasonable person. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Not Reported. (D302)
